name: Tour of Soule
description: Tackle the difficult 39.8-mile Tour of Soule road cycling route, gaining 5508 feet of elevation in about 4 hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Idaux-Mendy offers diverse landscapes, from rolling hills and expansive meadows to dense woods and fern-filled areas. The region is situated near the Pyrenees mountain range, providing panoramic views and varied terrain. Hikers can explore areas characterized by streams, ancient pollarded oaks, and geological features like the Arbailles karst massif and unique flysch hills. Best hiking trails around Idaux-Mendy The most popular hiking route is Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Ordiarp, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views of the Pyrenean chain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Madeleine Chapel loop from Sauguis-Saint-Étienne, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.4 km) path. This trail ascends through summer pastures and forests to the Madeleine, providing 360-degree panoramas of the Basque Country mountains and the Pyrenees. Local hikers also love the Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Pagolle, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Pyrenees, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Hiking around Idaux-Mendy is defined by rolling hills, dense woods, and views of the Pyrenean chain. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate paths, and more challenging treks. The routes in Idaux-Mendy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Idaux-Mendy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Idaux-Mendy?
A: There are over 50 hiking routes in the Idaux-Mendy area, offering a wide range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The komoot community has explored these trails over 500 times, providing valuable insights and ratings.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Idaux-Mendy?
A: Yes, Idaux-Mendy offers 17 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36008952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Pagolle&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.5 km trail that can be completed in under two hours, offering lovely views of the Pyrenees.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Idaux-Mendy?
A: The region around Idaux-Mendy boasts incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter rolling hills, expansive meadows, dense woods, and fern-filled areas dotted with ancient pollarded oaks. The terrain also features geological interests like the Arbailles karst massif and unique flysch hills, all set against the backdrop of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Many of the trails around Idaux-Mendy are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Ordiarp&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.2 km circular route offering expansive views of the Pyrenean chain.

Q: What are the best trails for scenic views of the Pyrenees?
A: For breathtaking panoramas, consider routes that ascend to higher points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madeleine Chapel loop from Sauguis-Saint-Étienne&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.4 km trail that leads to the Madeleine, providing magnificent 360-degree views of the Basque Country mountains and the Pyrenees. You can also find a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/797632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorama 360°&lt;/a&gt; highlight in the area.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see on the trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madeleine Chapel loop from Sauguis-Saint-Étienne&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3368769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madeleine Chapel&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the 'Randonnée Du Petit Train' (mentioned in region research) follows an old railroad line, combining history with adventure, though it's not explicitly listed in the guide data.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Idaux-Mendy?
A: The trails around Idaux-Mendy are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, from rolling hills and dense woods to stunning Pyrenean vistas, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails accessible by public transport?
A: While many trails start from villages like Idaux-Mendy, Ordiarp, or Sauguis-Saint-Étienne, specific public transport connections to trailheads can vary. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to these villages. For broader travel information in the region, you might find resources on &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme64.com/en/itinerary/idaux-mendy/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;tourisme64.com&lt;/a&gt; helpful.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging hikes in Idaux-Mendy?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult routes available. The 'Circuit Des Crêtes' (mentioned in region research) is a 15 km winding route offering a physical challenge and opportunities to observe local wildlife. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35285984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Chapelle Saint-Grégoire&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10.2 km.

Q: Can I find trails that pass through towns or villages?
A: Yes, some routes connect or pass through local communities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24647603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mauléon-Licharre loop from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.3 km trail that starts and ends in the town of Mauléon-Licharre, allowing you to explore the town itself (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5783570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt;).

Q: Are there any trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the 17 easy routes are suitable for families. The 'Le Circuit du Lac' (mentioned in region research) is an easy 5 km trail leading to a secluded lake, ideal for picnics and enjoying tranquility with children. These shorter, less strenuous paths are perfect for a family outing.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Idaux-Mendy?
A: The diverse landscapes of Idaux-Mendy make it enjoyable across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, while summer is ideal for higher altitude trails. Even winter hiking is possible, though some routes might require more preparation due to weather conditions.
tips:
Located in the heart of the Iraty Forest, the Bagargui Pass is reached via a beautiful 28-kilometer climb from Saint-Jean-le-Vieux. The climb averages 4%, with peaks reaching 11%. 
At the pass, you'll find the famous Iraty chalets and a grocery store for supplies.

Dangerous descent due to its steep ramps and long straights. It picks up a lot of speed and there are curves that require strong braking. On hot days you have to be even more careful because I have seen several blowouts due to the heat of the brakes.
Very pretty section along the Gave de Larrau. The road meanders along the curves of the river flowing in the bottom of the valley.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Tour of Soule by racebike?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike tour typically takes around 4 hours and 4 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Tour of Soule is rated as difficult. With an elevation gain of approximately 1679 meters over 64 kilometers, it requires a very good fitness level and is best suited for experienced racebikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Tour of Soule?
faqs.answer: You'll primarily be cycling on paved mountain roads, characteristic of the Pyrenees region. Expect significant climbs and descents, including mountain passes, which demand good handling skills. While the exact surface details aren't specified, roads in this region are generally well-maintained for cycling.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The Tour of Soule features several notable highlights. You'll pass through the scenic Bagargui mountain pass, experience the thrilling Descent of the Col d'Iraty, and cycle Along the Gave de Larrau, offering beautiful river views.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Soule a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: Based on the tour data, the Tour of Soule is designed as a loop, starting and ending at the same general location. This allows for a continuous cycling experience without needing to retrace your path.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Tour of Soule?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Pyrenees and the presence of mountain passes, the best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and mountain passes are clear of snow. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Tour of Soule cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebiking on public roads and mountain passes. While there aren't specific restrictions against dogs on the roads themselves, bringing a dog along on a demanding racebike tour of this nature is generally not practical or recommended for the animal's safety and comfort.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this tour?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle the Tour of Soule. It primarily uses public roads and mountain passes in the Soule region of the French Pyrenees. However, always respect local traffic laws and any specific regulations for protected natural areas you might pass through.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start the Tour of Soule?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, as a loop route in the Soule region, you can typically find parking in one of the towns or villages along the route, such as Larrau or Tardets-Sorholus, which are common starting points for Pyrenean cycling tours. Look for public parking areas in these locations.

faqs.question: Does the Tour of Soule intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers 40% of the Onsycol Bagargui trail, 21% of Le tour de Soule, and 20% of the HRP Variante 6.1. You'll also find it intersects with parts of La route des cols Hendaye - Larrau and Col de Bagargu.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation options along the Tour of Soule?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through the Soule region, you will find small villages and towns where you can stop for refreshments or find accommodation. Towns like Larrau, Tardets-Sorholus, or those near the Col d'Iraty often have cafes, restaurants, and guesthouses catering to cyclists and hikers. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially during peak season.
